proposed_relations.description: Both records refer to the identical Queensboro Bridge Bus Priority Study conducted by NYC DOT in 2010-2011. The approach_summary records the earlier 2010 community board presentation phase, while the summary_recommendations records the final October 2011 recommendations phase. Same study goal and project scope across both documents.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Queensboro Bridge Bus Priority Study: Summary of Recommendations
• Identify cost-effective ways to improve bus speed and reliability for local and express buses that use the QBB.
The Queensboro Bridge Bus Priority Study

rationale: The observation resolves to its own base_record_id project_qbb-bus-priority-study which already exists in the registry with the same source document (201110_qbb_summary_recommendations). Its identity is confirmed as new (matching itself). No different registry record matches this observation; the other Queensboro Bridge study record (project_queensboro-bridge-bus-priority-study) is from a different source (approach_summary) and represents the same real-world study but is a different record, linked via a proposed same_as relation rather than identity merge.

rationale: Observation resolves to its own base_record_id corridor_queensboro-bridge-qbb which already exists in the registry, confirming same source (201110_qbb_summary_recommendations), same corridor name (Queensboro Bridge), same boroughs (Manhattan, Queens), and same description (major bus corridor with 16,000 daily trips). Decision is new per canonicalization rules: the closest registry match is the observation's own base_record_id, so new is the correct decision.
